---
title: "Housing Market Data Loading/Cleaning"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-06"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
install.packages(c("tidyverse", "tidycensus", "censusapi", "readxl", "DBI", "RPostgres"))
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidycensus)
library(censusapi)
library(readxl)
library(DBI)
library(RPostgres)


#SETUP


#Census API key
census_api_key("6c3596bc22282d83e8771618f9a1730895b4b43e", install = TRUE, overwrite = TRUE)

# Connect to PostgreSQL
con <- dbConnect(
  RPostgres::Postgres(),
  dbname   = "postgres",
  host     = "localhost",
  port     = 5432,
  user     = "postgres",
  password = "Warriors123*"
)
```

```{r}
dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT schema_name FROM information_schema.schemata")
```
```{r}
#LOAD ZILLOW HOUSING PRICE DATA

#Read the Zillow ZHVI CSV
zillow_raw <- read_csv("Metro_zhvi_uc_sfrcondo_tier_0.33_0.67_sm_sa_month.csv")

#Peek at the structure
glimpse(zillow_raw)
head(zillow_raw)
```

```{r}
#CLEAN & RESHAPE ZILLOW DATA


zillow_clean <- zillow_raw %>%
  # Keep only metro areas, drop the national row
  filter(RegionType == "msa") %>%
  
  # Select only the columns we need
  select(RegionID, RegionName, StateName, starts_with("20")) %>%
  
  # Pivot from wide to long format (one row per metro per month)
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("20"),
    names_to = "date",
    values_to = "median_home_value"
  ) %>%
  
  # Parse the date column and extract year/month
  mutate(
    date  = as.Date(date),
    year  = year(date),
    month = month(date)
  ) %>%
  
  # Filter to our study window 2012-2023
  filter(year >= 2012 & year <= 2023) %>%
  
  # Drop rows with missing home values
  drop_na(median_home_value) %>%
  
  # Rename columns to match our database schema
  rename(
    cbsa_code  = RegionID,
    metro_name = RegionName,
    state      = StateName
  ) %>%
  
  # Convert cbsa_code to character to match CHAR(5) in postgres
  mutate(cbsa_code = as.character(cbsa_code))

# Check it looks right
glimpse(zillow_clean)
head(zillow_clean)
```

```{r}
# LOAD ZILLOW DATA INTO POSTGRESQL


# Step 1: Load metro_areas table first (unique metros only)
# We need this before housing_prices because of the foreign key relationship
metro_areas_insert <- zillow_clean %>%
  select(cbsa_code, metro_name, state) %>%
  distinct() %>%                        # one row per metro, no duplicates
  mutate(region = case_when(            # assign census region based on state
    state %in% c("CT","ME","MA","NH","RI","VT","NJ","NY","PA") ~ "Northeast",
    state %in% c("IL","IN","MI","OH","WI","IA","KS","MN","MO","NE","ND","SD") ~ "Midwest",
    state %in% c("DE","FL","GA","MD","NC","SC","VA","WV","AL","KY","MS","TN",
                 "AR","LA","OK","TX","DC") ~ "South",
    state %in% c("AZ","CO","ID","MT","NV","NM","UT","WY","AK","CA","HI","OR","WA") ~ "West",
    TRUE ~ "Unknown"
  ))

# Write metro_areas to PostgreSQL
dbWriteTable(
  con,
  name = Id(schema = "housing", table = "metro_areas"),
  value = metro_areas_insert,
  append = TRUE,     # append to existing table (don't overwrite)
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("metro_areas loaded:", nrow(metro_areas_insert), "rows\n")

# Step 2: Load housing_prices table
housing_prices_insert <- zillow_clean %>%
  select(cbsa_code, year, month, median_home_value)

dbWriteTable(
  con,
  name = Id(schema = "housing", table = "housing_prices"),
  value = housing_prices_insert,
  append = TRUE,
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("housing_prices loaded:", nrow(housing_prices_insert), "rows\n")
```


```{r}

# CLEAN & LOAD FREDDIE MAC MORTGAGE RATES


# Read the Excel file
mortgage_raw <- read_excel("historicalweeklydata.xlsx")

# Peek at it so we know what we're working with
glimpse(mortgage_raw)
head(mortgage_raw)
```

```{r}

# CLEAN & LOAD FREDDIE MAC MORTGAGE RATES


mortgage_clean <- mortgage_raw %>%
  # Only keep the date and 30yr rate columns
  select(date_raw = ...1, rate_raw = ...2) %>%
  
  # Drop the header rows and any non-data rows
  filter(!is.na(date_raw), !date_raw %in% c("Week", "")) %>%
  
  # Convert date (stored as Excel serial number) and rate to numeric
  mutate(
    date_raw  = as.numeric(date_raw),
    rate_raw  = as.numeric(rate_raw)
  ) %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  
  # Convert Excel serial date to actual date
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date_raw, origin = "1899-12-30"),
    year  = year(date),
    month = month(date),
    avg_30yr_rate = rate_raw
  ) %>%
  
  # Filter to our study window and average by month
  # (Freddie Mac is weekly so we average down to monthly)
  filter(year >= 2012 & year <= 2023) %>%
  group_by(year, month) %>%
  summarise(avg_30yr_rate = round(mean(avg_30yr_rate, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
            .groups = "drop")

# Check it
glimpse(mortgage_clean)
head(mortgage_clean)
```

```{r}
# Push mortgage rates to PostgreSQL
dbWriteTable(
  con,
  name = Id(schema = "housing", table = "mortgage_rates"),
  value = mortgage_clean,
  append = TRUE,
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("mortgage_rates loaded:", nrow(mortgage_clean), "rows\n")
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# PULL DEMOGRAPHICS FROM TIDYCENSUS (Census ACS 5-year estimates)
# Variables we want:
#   B01003_001 = total population
#   B19013_001 = median household income
#   B15003_022 = bachelor's degree holders (used to calc % college)
#   B15003_001 = total population 25+ (denominator for % college)
# ============================================================

# tidycensus can only pull one year at a time so we loop through 2012-2022
# (ACS 5-year estimates lag by ~1 year, 2023 not yet available)

years <- 2012:2022

demographics_raw <- map_dfr(years, function(yr) {
  get_acs(
    geography = "metropolitan statistical area/micropolitan statistical area",
    variables = c(
      population    = "B01003_001",
      median_income = "B19013_001",
      bachelors     = "B15003_022",
      pop_25plus    = "B15003_001"
    ),
    year    = yr,
    survey  = "acs5",
    output  = "wide"
  ) %>%
  mutate(year = yr)
})

glimpse(demographics_raw)
```

```{r}

# CLEAN & LOAD DEMOGRAPHICS DATA


demographics_clean <- demographics_raw %>%
  # Keep only the estimate columns, drop margin of error (M) columns
  select(
    cbsa_code     = GEOID,
    year,
    population    = populationE,
    median_income = median_incomeE,
    bachelors     = bachelorsE,
    pop_25plus    = pop_25plusE
  ) %>%
  
  # Calculate % college educated
  mutate(
    pct_college = round((bachelors / pop_25plus) * 100, 2)
  ) %>%
  
  # Drop the intermediate columns we no longer need
  select(-bachelors, -pop_25plus) %>%
  
  # Drop rows with missing key values
  drop_na(population, median_income)

# Push to PostgreSQL
dbWriteTable(
  con,
  name = Id(schema = "housing", table = "demographics"),
  value = demographics_clean,
  append = TRUE,
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("demographics loaded:", nrow(demographics_clean), "rows\n")
```



```{r}

# PULL UNEMPLOYMENT FROM TIDYCENSUS
# Variable: S2301_C04_001 = unemployment rate (ACS Subject Tables)

unemployment_raw <- map_dfr(years, function(yr) {
  get_acs(
    geography = "metropolitan statistical area/micropolitan statistical area",
    variables = c(unemployment_rate = "S2301_C04_001"),
    year   = yr,
    survey = "acs5",
    output = "wide"
  ) %>%
  mutate(year = yr)
})

# Clean it up
employment_clean <- unemployment_raw %>%
  select(
    cbsa_code         = GEOID,
    year,
    unemployment_rate = unemployment_rateE
  ) %>%
  drop_na(unemployment_rate) %>%
  # ACS is annual so we set month to 1 as a placeholder
  mutate(month = 1)

# Push to PostgreSQL
dbWriteTable(
  con,
  name = Id(schema = "housing", table = "employment"),
  value = employment_clean,
  append = TRUE,
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("employment loaded:", nrow(employment_clean), "rows\n")
```


```{r}

# FINAL DATA LOAD VERIFICATION


tables <- c("metro_areas", "housing_prices", "demographics", 
            "employment", "mortgage_rates")

for (t in tables) {
  count <- dbGetQuery(con, glue::glue(
    "SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM housing.{t}"
  ))
  cat(t, ":", count$n, "rows\n")
}
```


```{r}
dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM housing.metro_areas")
dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM housing.housing_prices")
dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM housing.demographics")
dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM housing.employment")
dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM housing.mortgage_rates")
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# RELOAD DEMOGRAPHICS WITH NAME COLUMN INCLUDED
# ============================================================

# First clear the existing demographics table
dbExecute(con, "TRUNCATE TABLE housing.demographics")

# Rebuild with name included
demographics_clean2 <- demographics_raw %>%
  select(
    cbsa_code     = GEOID,
    metro_name    = NAME,       # include the name this time
    year,
    population    = populationE,
    median_income = median_incomeE,
    bachelors     = bachelorsE,
    pop_25plus    = pop_25plusE
  ) %>%
  mutate(pct_college = round((bachelors / pop_25plus) * 100, 2)) %>%
  select(-bachelors, -pop_25plus) %>%
  drop_na(population, median_income)

# But first we need to add the column to the table in PostgreSQL
dbExecute(con, "ALTER TABLE housing.demographics ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS metro_name VARCHAR(200)")

# Now reload
dbWriteTable(
  con,
  name = Id(schema = "housing", table = "demographics"),
  value = demographics_clean2,
  append = TRUE,
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("demographics reloaded:", nrow(demographics_clean2), "rows\n")
```
